text.title: Context Window Curation Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every per-task context bundle must satisfy.

rule.statement: Bundle includes ONLY: target file(s), at most 2 callers (highest-coupling), referenced types/interfaces, AGENTS.md (or local equivalent), and glossary. No "just-in-case" additions.
rule.rationale: Each extra file raises latency + drift risk; the marginal benefit is near zero after 2 callers.

rule.statement: The bundle MUST include the most-specific applicable AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md). Without it, the agent re-invents conventions and drifts.
rule.rationale: AGENTS.md is the cheapest, highest-signal context; skipping it is the most-common adoption failure.

rule.statement: Total bundle ≤ 6000 tokens (configurable per project). Budget includes glossary, AGENTS.md, files, and the task description.
rule.rationale: Above 8K tokens, edit-time doubles and quality drops measurably on agent benchmarks.

rule.statement: A 100–200-token glossary of project-specific terms (domain words, internal acronyms, file-naming conventions) is included.
rule.rationale: Domain-specific terms anchored in glossary cut hallucinated terminology dramatically.

rule.statement: The bundle declares what is DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED ("not in this bundle: test fixtures, migrations, vendor/, generated/") so a reviewer can spot missing context fast.
rule.rationale: An exclusion record turns "you missed X" into a quick check rather than a deep audit.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (scoped task, repo + AGENTS.md, budget), execute: scope → pick target → pick callers → add types → add AGENTS.md → add glossary → budget check → emit bundle.
rule.rationale: Curation that skips the budget check is just hopeful selection.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) task is exploratory and crawl is the goal, (b) repo < 5K total lines (load everything), (c) cost / context-window are not constraints, (d) task is a deterministic codemod.
rule.rationale: Curation overhead is wasted when budget is unconstrained or scope is exploratory.
